mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in the federal funds rate lowers the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity present values and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns during months with declining FEDFUNDS do not exceed baseline by at least 2 percentage points annualized over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months reveals recession onset, widens the equity risk premium, and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances during any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in housing starts and retail sales expose broad household-sector deleveraging that crushes aggregate demand and earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint decline condition fails to produce negative 20-day returns in at least 60% of triggered months over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Annualized CPIAUCSL growth exceeding 3% during late-cycle expansions elevates real discount rates and reduces the present value of future corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances during any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production reflects a contraction in manufacturing output that forces broad earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO declines are positive more than 50% of the time over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate combined with a federal funds rate above 3% reflects restrictive monetary policy into a deteriorating labor market, amplifying recession risk and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of triggered quarters over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects sustained consumer spending strength that supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month RSAFS growth fails to positively correlate with 5-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDPC1 growth falling below 1% annualized reflects a broad demand slowdown that reduces corporate profit expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered quarters produce positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of instances over any 5-year rolling window.
